volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 4.2.1016c - Section 4.7.1124d)

sections.label: Section 4.2.1016c
sections.original: Sequor .—
sections.translation: I ’ m following .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1017
sections.original: Peiorem ego hominem magisque vorsute malum
sections.translation: I ’ troth , a more artful fellow , and one more skilfully cunning , I never did see than is this same Simmia . Very much do I dread this man , and sadly do I fear that

sections.label: Section 4.3.1020
sections.original: ne malus item erga me sit út erga illum fuit ,
sections.translation: he may prove mischievous against myself just as he has been against him ; lest in his prosperity he may now turn his horns against me , if he finds an opportunity . Should he prove mischievous towards me ⋯ But , i ’ faith , for my part I hope not , for I wish well to him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1024_1025
sections.original: nunc in metu sum maximo , triplici modo :
sections.translation: Now in three ways am I in the greatest dread . First of all then , I dread this comrade of mine , lest he should forsake me and go over from me to the enemy . Next do I dread that my master should in the meantime return from the Forum ; lest , the booty taken , the plunderers should be taken .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1030
sections.original: quom haec metuo , metuo ne ille huc Harpax advenat
sections.translation: Together with these things do I fear , lest that other Harpax should arrive here before this Harpax has departed hence with the woman . By my faith , I ’ m undone ; they are very slow in coming out of doors . With baggage packed [*] up , my heart is waiting , ready ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1034_1035
sections.original: si non educat mulierem secum simul ,
sections.translation: if he doesn ’ t bring out the damsel together with himself , to fly away in exile out from of my breast . ⋯ ( The door of BALLIO ’ S house opens .) I ’ m the conqueror — I ’ ve got the better of my wary guards .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1038
sections.original: Ne plora , nescis ut res sit , Phoenicium ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t weep : you know not how the matter is , Phœnicium ; but before long I ’ ll let you know it when you are resting .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1040
sections.original: non ego te ad illum duco dentatum virum
sections.translation: I ’ m not leading you to that Macedonian long-teethed fellow , who now causes you to be weeping . To him will I lead you , whose you especially long to be . I ’ ll cause you before very long to be embracing Calidorus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1044
sections.original: Quid tu intus quaeso desedisti quam diu ?
sections.translation: Prithee , why did you stay so long in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1045
sections.original: mihi cor retunsumst oppugnando pectore .
sections.translation: For how long a time was my heart throbbing with beating against my breast .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1046
sections.original: Occasionem repperisti , verbero ,
sections.translation: You have found reason , you whipping-post , with a vengeance to be making enquires of me ⋯ amid the ambush of the enemy . Why don ’ t we go home with all speed with military strides [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1049_1050
sections.original: Atque edepol , quamquam nequam homo es , recte mones .
sections.translation: Now , by my troth , although you are a worthless fellow , you advise aright . March in triumphal procession , this way , straight in the path to the festive goblet .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1052
sections.original: Hahae , nunc demum mi animus in tuto est loco ,
sections.translation: Ha , ha , ha ! ( laughing .) Now , at last , my mind is in a state of ease , since that fellow has departed hence , and taken away the woman . I should like Pseudolus now to come , that wicked rascal ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.1055
sections.original: et abdúcere a me mulierem fallaciis .
sections.translation: to carry the woman off from me by his stratagems . I know for sure , right well , that I had rather in solemn form perjure myself a thousand times , than that he should cheat me by making a laughing-stock of me . By my troth now , I ’ ll laugh at the fellow if I meet him .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1060
sections.original: verum in pistrino credo , ut convenit , fore .
sections.translation: But I guess that he ’ ll soon be on the treadmill , just as befits him . Now I wish for Simo that he would come in my way , in order that he might be joyful in common with my joy .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1063
sections.original: Viso quid rerum meus Vlixes egerit ,
sections.translation: I ’ m going to see what business my Ulysses has transacted ; whether he now has the statue [*] from the Ballionian citadel .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1065
sections.original: O fortunate , cedo fortunatam manum ,
sections.translation: O , lucky man ! give me your lucky hand .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1066b
sections.original: Quíd est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1066c
sections.original: Iam —
sections.translation: Now .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1066d
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: What now ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1066e
sections.original: Nihil est quod metuas .
sections.translation: There ’ s nothing at all for you to fear .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1066f
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? Has that fellow Pseudolus come to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1067b
sections.original: Non .
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1067c
sections.original: Quid est igitur boni ?
sections.translation: What good fortune is there , then ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1068
sections.original: Minae viginti sanae et salvae sunt tibi ,
sections.translation: Your twenty minae are safe and sound which Pseudolus stipulated for from you this day .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1070
sections.original: Velim quidem hercle .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really do wish they were .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1070b
sections.original: Róga me viginti minas ,
sections.translation: Ask of me twenty minae if he this day gets hold of that woman , or gives her to your son this day , as he has promised . On my word , prithee , do demand them of me ; I quite long to promise them .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1074
sections.original: omnibus modis tibi esse rem ut salvam scias ;
sections.translation: And , besides this , keep the woman as a present for yourself , that in every way you may know that your money ’ s safe .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1076
sections.original: Nullum periclumst , quod sciam , stipularier ,
sections.translation: There ’ s no danger that I know of in making this bargain . According as you have solemnly pledged your word , will you give me twenty minae ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1078b
sections.original: Dabuntur .
sections.translation: They shall be given .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1078c
sections.original: Hoc quidem actumst hau male .
sections.translation: This , indeed , is not so badly done . But have you met the fellow ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1079b
sections.original: Immo ambo simul .
sections.translation: Aye , both of them together .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1080
sections.original: Quid ait ? quid narrat ? quaeso , quid dixit tibi ?
sections.translation: What did he say ? What did ho talk about ? Prithee , what did he mention to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1081
sections.original: Nugas theatri , verba quae in comoediis
sections.translation: Theatrical nonsense ; expressions which , in comedies , are wont to be used to a procurer , which boys are acquainted with . He said that I was worthless , and wicked , and forsworn .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1084
sections.original: Pol hau mentitust .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he told no lie .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1084b
sections.original: Ergo haud iratus fui :
sections.translation: ’ Twas for that reason I wasn ’ t angry .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1085
sections.original: nam quanti refert ei nec recte dicere ,
sections.translation: For what matters it for you to speak uncivilly to him who cares not for it , and who don ’ t deny the truth of what you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1087
sections.original: Quid est ? quid non metuam ab eo ? id audire expeto .
sections.translation: Why is it that you are in no fear of him ? That I ’ m longing to hear .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1088
sections.original: Quia numquam abducet mulierem iam , nec potest ,
sections.translation: Because he never will carry the woman off now , nor is he able . Don ’ t you yourself remember that I told you , some time since ,

sections.label: Section 4.6.1090
sections.original: eam véniisse militi Macedonio ?
sections.translation: that she had been sold to a Macedonian officer ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1091
sections.original: Memini .
sections.translation: I remember .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1091b
sections.original: Em íllius servos húc ad me argentum attulit
sections.translation: Well , his servant just now brought me the money , and the token with the impression , which had been arranged between himself and me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1092b
sections.original: Quid postea ?
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1093
sections.original: Qui inter me atque illum militem convenerat :
sections.translation: He took away with him the woman , not long since .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1095
sections.original: Bonan fide istuc dicis ?
sections.translation: Do you say this in real truth ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1095b
sections.original: Vnde ea sit mihi ?
sections.translation: Whence could I possibly have that quality ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1096
sections.original: Vide módo , ne illic sit contechnatus quippiam .
sections.translation: Do you only take care that he hasn ’ t been playing some trick there .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1097
sections.original: Epistula atque imago me certum facit ;
sections.translation: The letter and the impress on the seal make me sure . Indeed , ’ twas but just now he took her off for Sicyon .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1099
sections.original: Bene hercle factum . quid ego cesso Pseudolum
sections.translation: Troth now , ’ twas well done . Why do I delay to make Pseudolus give a name [*] to a colony at the mill-stones ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1101
sections.original: sed quis hic homo est chlamydatus ?
sections.translation: But who ’ s this fellow in the scarf ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1101b
sections.original: Non edepol scio ,
sections.translation: I don ’ t know , i ’ faith ; however , let ’ s observe whither he ’ s going , and what business he ’ s upon .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1103
sections.original: Malus ét nequam est homo quí nihili eri ímperium sui sérvos facit ,
sections.translation: That slave ’ s a base and worthless fellow , who values his master ’ s commands at nought ; and he , too , is good for nothing who is forgetful to do his duty unless he ’ s put in mind .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1105
sections.original: nam quí liberós esse ilicó se arbitrantur ,
sections.translation: But those who forthwith deem themselves to be at liberty , when they have hidden themselves from the sight of their master , who riot , wench , devour what they have ,— aye , what they have not ,— long do those same endure the name of servitude . Nor is there any good disposition in them ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.1110
sections.original: nisi ut improbis se artibus teneant . †
sections.translation: except only that they may uphold themselves by their dishonest contrivances . With these , neither their company nor their conversation suits me , nor by these persons have I been ever known . Although he is away , I consider my master as being here ; I fear him

sections.label: Section 4.7.1115
sections.original: quom hic nón adest , ne quom ádsiet métuam . ei rei operám dabo .
sections.translation: when he isn ’ t here , that I may not have to fear him when he is here . Therefore , as I have been ordered , to this business will I give my attention . But that Syrus , to whom I gave the token , would have been letting me stay even yet in the shop . As he bade me , I stayed there ; he said that he would send for me when the procurer was at home . But since he hasn ’ t come , or called me while staying there ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.1120
sections.original: venio húc ultro , ut sciam , quíd rei sit , ne illíc homo me ludíficetur .
sections.translation: I ’ m come hither of my own accord , that I may know what is the fact , that that fellow mayn ’ t be playing tricks with me . And there is nothing better than that I should knock at this door , and call some out here from indoors . I want this procurer to take this money of me , and to send off this woman with me .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1124
sections.original: Heús tu .
sections.translation: Hark you !

sections.label: Section 4.7.1124b
sections.original: Quid vis ?
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1124c
sections.original: Híc homo meus est .
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s my own .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1124d
sections.original: Quídum ?
sections.translation: How so ?
